author: geunhwan
title: Docker buildx builder boot fails - buildkit image not found in Azure Container Registry
signature: docker buildx builder boot fails no such image buildkit|Infrastructure

failed_jobs.name: Docker_arm64
failed_jobs.symptom: Docker buildx bootstrap fails: No such image openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io/dockerio/moby/buildkit:buildx-stable-1

key_errors: #1 ERROR: Error response from daemon: No such image: openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io/dockerio/moby/buildkit:buildx-stable-1
##[error]ERROR: Error response from daemon: No such image: openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io/dockerio/moby/buildkit:buildx-stable-1
root_cause: The Docker buildx builder could not boot because the required buildkit image (openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io/dockerio/moby/buildkit:buildx-stable-1) was not found in the Azure Container Registry mirror. This is an infrastructure/registry issue unrelated to the PR code changes. The image pull appeared to start (0.2s done message) but then creating the container failed with 'No such image', suggesting a transient race condition or temporary registry unavailability.
reproduction_steps: N/A - not reproducible outside CI as it requires access to the internal Azure Container Registry.
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed Docker_arm64 job - this is a transient infrastructure failure
If the issue persists, check the health of openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io registry and the buildkit image availability
Consider adding retry logic to the docker buildx create/bootstrap step
prevention_strategies: Add retry logic to the Docker buildx builder bootstrap step. Consider using a public mirror as fallback for the buildkit image.
historical_context: A similar Azure Container Registry issue was seen in run 29410904250 (2026-07-15) where OAuth token fetching was refused. Both failures are infrastructure-related registry connectivity issues.
